response:
"What would an innocent person say after he falsely received a verdict from the court?"

SECTION 1  
An innocent person, upon receiving a false verdict, might express confusion and disbelief. They could say something like, "This can't be right—how could such a mistake happen?" The emotional weight of the moment would likely lead to a mix of anger and helplessness. A silver lining might be the opportunity to fight for justice, though the process could feel overwhelming. The person might also feel a strong urge to seek support from those who believe in their innocence.

SECTION 2  
Sir, I am truly baffled by this turn of events. It feels as though the truth has been wrapped in a thick layer of misunderstanding. A stranger approached me earlier, claiming to have evidence that could change everything. I was told to lead the way to a new beginning, but all I see is a path filled with uncertainty. The silver lining, if there is one, is that I now have a chance to prove my innocence. I will not let this verdict define me.
